nodes.label: graphify query/path/explain workflow
nodes.rationale: Use graphify query/path/explain first for repo questions because it returns a scoped subgraph, smaller than full report or raw grep output

nodes.label: totalParts invariant
nodes.rationale: totalParts = components.length for full circles, components.length - 1 for partial arcs so the last component lands exactly on startAngle + sweepAngle

nodes.label: ADR-0001: Imperative ref API for show/hide
nodes.rationale: Show/hide exposed via useImperativeHandle ref (CircleLayoutRef.showComponents/hideComponents) rather than a prop-driven visible boolean, because consumers trigger show/hide from discrete events (gesture/button press) not derived state; matches React Native idiom like FlatList.scrollToIndex, TextInput.focus. Tradeoff: a visible prop would require parent to own boolean state and cause full reconciler diff/re-render of CircleLayout and all CircleLayoutComponent children on every toggle.
nodes.norm_label: adr-0001: imperative ref api for show/hide

nodes.label: ADR-0002: totalParts invariant for partial arcs
nodes.rationale: totalParts = components.length for full circles (sweepAngle === 2π), but components.length - 1 for partial arcs. Each component placed at angle startAngle + sweepAngle * (index/totalParts). For full circle, totalParts=n avoids overlapping first/last component and naturally closes the circle. For partial arc, totalParts=n-1 ensures the last component lands exactly on startAngle + sweepAngle (the arc endpoint); using n would leave the endpoint empty. Consequence: any per-component angle computation must use totalParts from context, not components.length directly; adding/removing components reassigns totalParts and repositions all components.
nodes.norm_label: adr-0002: totalparts invariant for partial arcs
